TSLA: Tesla Earnings: Shares Fall Slightly as Management Raises Capital Expenditure Guidance. Tesla's first-quarter earnings showed strong year-over-year operating profits and free cash flow, but shares fell slightly after management raised 2026 capital expenditure guidance to over $25 billion, tripling 2025 levels. This significant investment is directed towards the company's transition to autonomous vehicles and humanoid robots, which Morningstar views as a positive long-term strategy given the progress in these areas. Despite the increased capex and negative free cash flow guidance for the remainder of the year, Morningstar maintains a fair value estimate of $400 for Tesla, advising investors to await a greater margin of safety before buying.

Musk says Tesla, SpaceX to use Intel’s 14A tech in Terafab, lifting Intel shares (INTC:NASDAQ). Elon Musk announced that Tesla and SpaceX will utilize Intel's 14A technology for their "Terafab" project. This news provided a boost to Intel's manufacturing revival efforts, with Intel shares rising approximately 3% in postmarket trading following the announcement.

Electric vehicle test, cancer support part of ArcBest ‘Impact Report’. ArcBest has released its 2025 Impact Report, detailing environmental programs and initiatives focused on employee well-being and corporate responsibility. Key highlights include a successful three-week test of a Tesla Class 8 electric truck, along with various efforts in waste reduction, renewable energy, and facility upgrades. The report also outlines new employee benefits such as a cancer support program and updated dental coverage.

iShares MSCI World ETF's Tech-Heavy Bet Faces a Critical Week. The iShares MSCI World ETF, heavily weighted in technology, faces a critical week as key tech companies like Tesla, Apple, and Microsoft report earnings. Tesla's recent struggles put pressure on the ETF, which allocates a significant portion to the information technology sector. Additionally, a major overhaul of MSCI's index methodology in May 2026 is expected to cause significant portfolio adjustments for the fund.

This announcement highlights the ETF's strategy of generating income, likely through options trading strategies related to Tesla stock. Investors in TSLY can expect to receive this distribution.

Today’s Market Recap: Nasdaq Hits Record High as Tech Rallies on AI Growth; Tesla Q1 Beats Estimates Amid Spending Surge. The Nasdaq Composite achieved a new record high, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq seeing significant gains driven by mega-cap tech stocks, Alphabet, and semiconductor companies benefiting from AI demand. Tesla's Q1 results showed strong revenue and earnings growth, though the company plans for substantial capital expenditures. Meanwhile, Google and OpenAI advanced AI with new chips and tools, while broader markets reacted to potential ceasefire talks between the U.S. and Iran and a political dispute over tariff refunds.

Tesla increases spending plans as company reports positive free cash flow. Tesla has significantly raised its spending forecast to over US$25-billion for the year, focusing on AI, robotics, and芯片 to boost future revenue streams. Despite investor skepticism causing a stock dip, the company reported a positive free cash flow of US$1.44-billion in the first quarter, surprising analysts who had expected a cash burn. Tesla is accelerating its plans for autonomous Cybercabs and robotaxis, and continues to develop a cheaper electric SUV, though its core automotive business faces pressure from competitors.

Tesla's Musk says company plans to use Intel's 14A process for Terafab. Tesla CEO Elon Musk announced that the company intends to utilize Intel's advanced 14A manufacturing process for its Terafab project, which aims to produce chips for AI, robotics, and data centers. Tesla and SpaceX have joined Intel's Terafab AI chip complex, with Musk expressing confidence in the 14A process's maturity and Intel's relationship. This move could bolster investor confidence in Intel's next-generation manufacturing, given its past struggles with chip yield.

Tesla Expands Cybertruck V2G Capabilities to California to Help Stabilize Electrical Grid. Tesla is expanding its Cybertruck Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) and Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) capabilities to California, allowing Cybertruck owners in PG&E's service territory to use their vehicles to support the electrical grid and earn money. This initiative, called Powershare Grid Support, requires specific hardware including the Cybertruck, a Tesla Powershare Gateway, and a Tesla Universal Wall Connector. This expansion follows a successful launch in Texas and aims to use EVs as mobile power banks to stabilize grids and provide backup power.

PG&E Approves Tesla Cybertruck For First-Of-Its-Kind Grid Program. PG&E has approved the Tesla Cybertruck and its charging equipment for a new residential Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) program in California, allowing customers to power homes and sell electricity back to the grid. This initiative, utilizing Tesla's Powershare technology, is the state's first AC-based vehicle-to-grid system and aims to promote EV adoption as flexible energy assets. The article also provides a technical analysis of PG&E's stock, its company context, upcoming earnings, and analyst outlook.
SBUX: Boston Trust Walden Corp Sells 12,058 Shares of Starbucks Corporation $SBUX. Boston Trust Walden Corp reduced its stake in Starbucks Corporation by 6.6% in Q4, selling 12,058 shares and finishing the quarter with 169,644 shares valued at approximately $14.29 million. Starbucks recently reported Q4 EPS of $0.56, missing estimates, but revenue rose to $9.92 billion, exceeding consensus, and declared a quarterly dividend of $0.62. The article also highlights recent insider selling, institutional ownership, and analyst ratings for Starbucks.

Seattle could lose hundreds of millions in tax revenue as Starbucks expands in Tennessee. This article discusses the potential financial impact on Seattle as Starbucks expands its operations in Tennessee. Seattle could reportedly lose hundreds of millions in tax revenue due to Starbucks' increased presence outside of the state. The move highlights the ongoing trend of companies relocating or expanding in states with more favorable tax environments.

Starbucks Hunts for Another Tech Office, Including Abroad. Starbucks is actively seeking a new location for its technology division, contemplating both domestic and international cities, to further reduce tech-related expenses. This initiative follows the company's recent announcement of a new office in Nashville and a reported $100 million in tech spending cuts over the last three months, as disclosed by CTO Anand Varadarajan. The move aims to optimize operational costs for the coffee chain.

Seattle could lose hundreds of millions in tax revenue as Starbucks expands in Tennessee. Seattle faces a potential loss of up to $750 million in tax revenue as Starbucks announced a $100 million investment and 2,000 new jobs in Nashville, Tennessee. This move comes as Seattle's Tax Foundation ranking for doing business has plummeted and the state recently implemented a "millionaires tax," contrasting sharply with Tennessee Governor Bill Lee's welcoming stance. Seattle Mayor Katie Wilson's past encouragement to boycott Starbucks also highlights the differing business climates between the two states.

Starbucks will use part of Asurion's Gulch HQ for its temporary home. Starbucks, which recently announced a 2,000-job corporate hub in Nashville, will temporarily occupy office space in Asurion's Gulch headquarters. This interim arrangement will last until Starbucks's 250,000 square feet of office space is ready. The move highlights the ongoing expansion of major companies into Nashville.

NKE: Lululemon shares drop as new CEO appointment leaves investors unimpressed. Lululemon Athletica's shares dropped 4% after naming former Nike executive Heidi O'Neill as its new CEO. Investors and analysts were reportedly unimpressed with the appointment, preferring activist investor Elliott Investment Management's choice of retail veteran Jane Nielsen. This comes as Lululemon navigates weak sales, increased competition, and an ongoing proxy fight with its founder, Chip Wilson.

Tim Cook May Have Bought Nike Stock, But The Sneaker Giant Faces Potential 'Generational Reset,' Says This Analyst. Nike Inc. is experiencing one of its most significant stock drawdowns, leading analysts to suggest a potential "generational reset" for the brand. Despite this, insider investments, including purchases by Apple CEO Tim Cook and Nike CEO Elliott Hill, signal confidence in the company's long-term value. While Nike beat revenue and earnings expectations in its recent fiscal third quarter, a weak fourth-quarter sales forecast negatively impacted its stock price and led to lowered analyst target prices.

Former Nike executive to take over as Lululemon's new CEO. Lululemon has appointed former Nike executive Heidi O'Neill as its new CEO, effective September 8. This decision comes after months of shareholder pressure and leadership changes, following a turbulent period for the athleisure retailer. O'Neill's experience at Nike is expected to help Lululemon address declining share prices, increased competition, and the need for product innovation.

Lululemon names former Nike exec Heidi O’Neill as next CEO. Lululemon Athletica Inc. has appointed former Nike executive Heidi O’Neill as its new chief executive officer, effective September 8. This decision comes after months of criticism regarding the company's management and performance, including pressure from founder Chip Wilson. O'Neill is expected to lead the athleisure retailer through increased competition and efforts to revitalize its product innovation and market presence.

Lululemon names former Nike exec Heidi O’Neill as next CEO. O'Neill's extensive experience at Nike, where she significantly contributed to its growth, is expected to help Lululemon address mounting competition and revitalize its brand. Lululemon names former Nike executive O’Neill its next CEO. Lululemon Athletica Inc. has appointed Heidi O’Neill, former Nike Inc. president of consumer, product and brand, as its new chief executive officer, effective September 8. O'Neill takes the helm during a challenging period for Lululemon, marked by slowing growth, product issues, and investor concerns, including a significant stake by Elliott Investment Management and public criticism from founder Chip Wilson. Lululemon names former Nike exec Heidi O’Neill as next CEO. Lululemon Athletica Inc. has appointed Heidi O'Neill, a former Nike executive, as its new chief executive officer, effective September 8th. O'Neill will also join the company's board of directors. This change comes after shareholder criticism regarding Lululemon's share price and sales performance, following the previous CEO's resignation in December.

Lululemon names former Nike exec Heidi O’Neill as next CEO. Lululemon Athletica Inc. has appointed former Nike executive Heidi O’Neill as its next chief executive officer, effective September 8. NIKE (NKE) CEO boosts stake with April open-market share buy. NIKE, Inc.'s President & CEO, Elliott Hill, acquired 23,660.235 shares of Class B Common Stock on April 13, 2026, at a weighted average price of $42.27 per share. This open-market purchase increased his direct holdings to 265,247.235 NIKE Class B shares. The amended Form 4/A was filed to clarify the weighted average price resulting from multiple trades.

Lululemon says it picks former Nike executive as next CEO. Lululemon Athletica has named Heidi O'Neill, a former top Nike executive, as its new chief executive, replacing Calvin McDonald. The appointment comes as the athletic apparel retailer faces declining sales and pressure from investors to revitalize its business. Lululemon's stock price has fallen by 38% in the past year due to competition and shifts in consumer preferences.

Lululemon Names Former Nike Executive O’Neill Its Next CEO. Lululemon Athletica Inc. has appointed Heidi O’Neill, formerly Nike Inc.'s president of consumer, product and brand, as its new chief executive officer. O'Neill will assume the role on September 8, succeeding Calvin McDonald who departed in January. This leadership change comes as the athletic retailer aims to overcome a period of slowing growth and investor concerns.

Lululemon appoints long-time Nike executive Heidi O’Neill as new CEO. Lululemon Athletica Inc. has appointed former Nike executive Heidi O’Neill as its new CEO, effective Sept. 8, following a four-month search. Ms. O’Neill will be tasked with reinvigorating the brand amidst declining stock prices, competition, and concerns about stagnation, particularly in North America where sales have been faltering. Her extensive experience at Nike and commitment to product innovation and cultural relevance are expected to address Lululemon's current challenges and drive global growth.

Nike veteran Heidi O’Neill will take over as lululemon CEO. Lululemon Athletica Inc. (NASDAQ: LULU) has appointed Heidi O’Neill, a seasoned Nike veteran, as its new Chief Executive Officer, effective September 8, 2026. O'Neill brings over three decades of experience in the performance apparel, footwear, and sport industry, having previously served in various leadership roles at Nike, where she played a key role in growing the company. Interim co-CEOs Meghan Frank and André Maestrini will continue to lead until O’Neill’s arrival, after which they will return to their previous senior leadership positions.
